Ceramide NP replenishes the skin's natural lipid matrix to restore and strengthen the epidermal barrier — essential for reducing transepidermal water loss and protecting against irritants. Sodium Hyaluronate (high MW) and Sodium Hyaluronate Crosspolymer (crosslinked HA) provide surface and extended film hydration. Squalane and Simmondsia Chinensis (Jojoba) Seed Oil provide lightweight emolliency. Borago Officinalis (Borage) Seed Oil is rich in GLA (gamma-linolenic acid) for barrier repair. Saccharide Isomerate adds long-duration moisture retention. Mandelic Acid provides very mild exfoliation and texture improvement. Rich cream texture. Paraben-free. Note: contains Myristyl Myristate (comedogenicity 5/5 — not safe for fungal acne).
how_to_use: Apply to cleansed and toned skin. Spread evenly over face. AM and PM. Layer under SPF in AM.

name: Pitstop Gel
description: Re'equil's clinically trialled spot treatment gel for acne-induced post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation (PIH), pitted scars, and atrophic marks. The formula pairs Allium Cepa (Onion Bulb) Extract — clinically validated for softening scar tissue, reducing inflammation, and improving the appearance of scars — with Bulbine Frutescens Leaf Juice, a South African succulent with wound-healing and skin-regenerating properties analogous to aloe vera but richer in glycoproteins. Bellis Perennis (Daisy) Flower Extract provides gentle brightening. Sodium Hyaluronate hydrates the treatment area. Allantoin and Panthenol soothe and support healing. Based on clinical trials on 34 subjects with PIH and atrophic acne scars. Apply 3–4x daily. Paraben-free.
how_to_use: Apply a thin layer directly to acne scars, pits, and marks 3–4 times daily. Use for 8–10 weeks on new scars and 3–6 months on existing scars.

name: Acne Clarifying Gel
description: Re'equil's active acne spot and area treatment gel combining Glycolic Acid (AHA) and Salicylic Acid (BHA) in a rich botanical complex. Glycolic Acid (AHA) dissolves intercellular bonds between dead cells at the skin surface, while Salicylic Acid (BHA) penetrates oil-filled pores to exfoliate from within and reduce comedone formation. Salix Alba (White Willow) Bark Extract provides natural salicin with anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. Oligopeptide-10 is an antimicrobial peptide that reduces acne-causing bacteria (P. acnes) counts. Boswellia Serrata Resin Extract provides potent anti-inflammatory action. Scutellaria Baicalensis, Houttuynia Cordata and Phellodendron Amurense add further soothing and anti-acne botanical support. Rich emollient base means the gel does not over-dry skin. Paraben-free.

name: Skin Radiance Cream
description: Re'equil's dermatologist-recommended multi-active depigmentation cream targeting hyperpigmentation, dark spots, melasma, age spots, and sun spots across multiple melanogenesis pathways simultaneously. Niacinamide (Vitamin B3) blocks melanin transfer from melanocytes to keratinocytes and refines skin texture. Tetrahydrocurcumin (White Curcumin — a hydrogenated, colourless derivative of curcumin) inhibits tyrosinase and reduces melanin overproduction. Arbutin (natural hydroquinone derivative) inhibits tyrosinase at a different enzymatic step. Diacetyl Boldine (from Peumus boldus) inhibits both tyrosinase and melanocyte stimulation. Tetrahydropiperine enhances the bioavailability and skin penetration of co-actives. Pterocarpus Marsupium Bark Extract and Glycyrrhiza Glabra (Licorice) provide additional anti-inflammatory and brightening botanicals. Allergen-free fragrance. Paraben-free. Clinical trials on 36 subjects. Visible results in 4 weeks.
how_to_use: Massage a button-sized amount over face, neck, and back of hands. AM and PM for a minimum 6–8 weeks. Always follow with SPF in AM.

name: Hair Fall Control Shampoo
description: Re'equil's sulphate-free shampoo targeting hair fall from weakened follicles. Powered by two clinically validated hair loss actives: Cressatine (a patented blend of Indian Cress extract Tropaeolum Majus and Watercress extract Nasturtium Officinale — a natural source of glucosinolates that stimulate follicle growth and increase hair fibre diameter) and Aminexil (a patented IPCA molecule that prevents collagen rigidification around the hair follicle — the leading cause of follicle miniaturisation and permanent hair loss). name: Damage Repair Conditioner
description: Re'equil's deep repair conditioner for chemically treated, heat-damaged, or fragile hair. name: Candid Dusting Powder
description: Glenmark's flagship OTC antifungal dusting powder — India's undisputed #1 antifungal dusting powder for over three decades, with the highest prescription rate of any dermatology OTC brand (IMS ranking). Contains Clotrimazole 1% — an imidazole antifungal that inhibits ergosterol synthesis in fungal cell membranes by blocking the CYP51 enzyme (lanosterol 14α-demethylase), disrupting the structural integrity and permeability of the fungal cell membrane. Ergosterol is fungal cell membranes' equivalent of cholesterol — its depletion kills the fungal cells. Effective against dermatophytes (ringworm, athlete's foot, jock itch), Candida (intertrigo, nappy rash), and Malassezia (tinea versicolor). The powder format is ideal for India's hot and humid climate — absorbs sweat, prevents skin maceration in body folds, and delivers antifungal protection to moisture-prone areas that are most susceptible to fungal infection. Paraben-free.
how_to_use: Dust a thin layer over the affected area and surrounding skin. AM and PM. Continue for 2 weeks after symptoms resolve.

name: Discipline Bain Fluidealiste Anti-Frizz Shampoo
description: Kérastase's iconic anti-frizz shampoo from the Discipline range — the India bestseller within the Discipline line and one of the brand's most recognised products for frizzy and unruly hair. Formulated with the proprietary Morpho-Kératine™ Complex — Kérastase's unique adaptation of a biomimetic concept derived from the structural arrangement of scales in the Morpho butterfly wing, which produces its iridescent blue colour through microstructure rather than pigment. The complex is a combination of amino acids, wheat protein extracts, and ceramide-R that coats the hair fibre in a uniform film, restoring its surface homogeneity — the key to frizz control. Frizz occurs when hair fibres have an irregular, raised cuticle surface that allows atmospheric moisture to enter unevenly; Morpho-Kératine™ creates a smooth, sealed surface that prevents hygroscopic swelling. description: Kérastase's luxury anti-dandruff shampoo from the Symbiose range — the brand's entry into the medicated scalp care segment with a formulation that maintains Kérastase's premium sensorial positioning. Symbiose targets dandruff through a dual mechanism: Zinc Pyrithione (ZPT) as the antifungal active specifically targeting Malassezia overgrowth — the fungal cause of dandruff; and Salicylic Acid for keratolytic exfoliation of the adherent dandruff scales. name: Première Bain Décalcifiant Réparateur Shampoo
description: Kérastase's breakthrough decalcifying repair shampoo from the Première range — addressing the under-recognised source of hair damage in India's hard water cities (Delhi, Mumbai, Bangalore): calcium deposits. Hard water contains high concentrations of dissolved calcium and magnesium carbonate that deposit on the hair surface with every wash, building a mineral scale that progressively stiffens, dulls, and weakens hair fibres. Over months, hard water scale accumulates on the cuticle surface, increasing friction, reducing shine, and preventing active ingredients in conditioners and masks from penetrating the fibre. Pure Citric Acid — the hero active in Première — is the hair industry's most effective chelating and decalcifying agent, dissolving calcium carbonate scale from the hair surface without damaging the cuticle. Première's breakthrough is delivering Citric Acid in a stable shampoo format that releases it during cleansing to simultaneously dissolve scale while washing. Strengthening actives (Citric Acid + Bond Repair Technology) then immediately begin fibre repair. The Décalcifiant Réparateur (Decalcifying Repairing) name precisely describes the dual action. Sulfate-free.
how_to_use: Wet hair. Apply, massage thoroughly. For best results leave on for 3–5 minutes before rinsing to allow Citric Acid to dissolve calcium deposits. Rinse. Use at every wash.

concerns: {hard water damage, calcium deposits, dullness, stiffness, hair damage, breakage, scale build-up}

name: 5% AHA Underarm Roll-On Fragrance-Free
description: Chemist at Play's fragrance-free 5% AHA underarm roll-on — one of India's first AHA-based targeted underarm treatment products and the brand's signature product that established the category. The roll-on format delivers liquid formulation precisely to the underarm zone using a ball applicator, providing controlled leave-on AHA treatment without the mess of a cream or the waste of a spray. The 5% AHA blend — Lactic Acid and Mandelic Acid — exfoliates the hyper-pigmented underarm skin through the same keratolytic mechanism that AHAs exert on face skin: dissolving the corneodesmosomes that hold pigmented dead skin cells together, accelerating their shedding and revealing fresher, lighter skin beneath. Lactic Acid (smaller molecule) provides faster surface exfoliation. Mandelic Acid (larger molecule) penetrates more slowly for gentler deeper exfoliation. Niacinamide provides concurrent melanin transfer inhibition — reducing the signal from melanocytes to keratinocytes that deposits melanin. The combination addresses both the accumulated pigmentation (AHA exfoliation) and the ongoing pigmentation mechanism (Niacinamide). Ceramides repair the skin barrier that shaving and friction progressively damage in the underarm zone. The fragrance-free variant is specifically for those with sensitive underarm skin or fragrance sensitivities. Brand position: India's 1st ceramide-based skincare, all products vegan, paraben-free, silicone-free.

name: Gentle Soothing Face Wash 5% Vitamin B5
description: Chemist at Play's gentle face wash for sensitive, reactive, and barrier-compromised skin — the most delicate formula in the brand's face wash range. Formulated with 5% Vitamin B5 (Panthenol — a provitamin that converts to Pantothenic Acid in skin and is critical for skin barrier protein synthesis), Zemea (Propanediol, gentle humectant and penetration enhancer), and Trehalose (a disaccharide from desiccation-resistant organisms that acts as a cryoprotectant and humectant). Panthenol at 5% is the functional range for skin barrier repair — replenishing the skin's natural barrier protein infrastructure. Trehalose is particularly valuable for sensitive skin: it forms a protective film of water around skin cells during desiccation stress (hot water washing, low humidity environments), preventing cell membrane damage. The formula is soap-free, sulfate-free, and fragrance-free — removing all the common sensitising agents present in conventional face washes. For daily use on sensitive, dry, eczema-prone, rosacea, or post-procedure skin that cannot tolerate any actives.

name: Intensely Hydrating Face Moisturizer
description: Chemist at Play's signature face moisturiser — the brand's foundational hydration product built on its core identity as India's 1st ceramide-based skincare brand. Formulated with Ceramides (specifically NP, AP, and EOP — the three essential ceramide types that constitute the majority of the skin's barrier lipid matrix) and Hyaluronic Acid as the two-layer hydration system. Ceramides are phospholipids that form the structural 'mortar' between the corneocyte 'bricks' of the skin barrier — without adequate ceramides, the barrier becomes porous, allowing transepidermal water loss (TEWL) and entry of irritants. The skin's ceramide content decreases with age, environmental damage, harsh cleansing, and active ingredient use (particularly retinoids and AHAs). Supplementing with topical ceramides from a functionally identical molecular size to the skin's own replenishes the barrier matrix. Hyaluronic Acid (Sodium Hyaluronate) fills the spaces between cells with hydration, complementing the barrier's sealing function of ceramides. The combination — Ceramides seal the barrier, HA provides intracellular hydration — is the optimal two-mechanism moisturising architecture. Lightweight enough for daily use including under SPF. Suitable for all skin types including oily.
how_to_use: Apply to cleansed face morning and evening. Use after toner and serum steps. Can be used under SPF in the morning.

description: Chemist at Play's flagship Vitamin C face serum — the brand's most popular face serum and a key product in the Indian prestige vitamin C market. name: 1% Hyaluronic Acid Gel Sunscreen SPF 50+ PA++++
description: Chemist at Play's SPF 50+ PA++++ gel sunscreen — the brand's daily sun protection product combining 1% Hyaluronic Acid (the highest HA concentration in a commercial Indian sunscreen) with a broad-spectrum chemical UV filter system. The gel texture is critical: the single largest barrier to sunscreen compliance in India is cosmetic elegance — heavy, white-casting, greasy sunscreens are avoided despite knowledge of their importance. The gel format is virtually weightless, absorbs in under 60 seconds, leaves zero white cast, and can be worn comfortably under makeup. The 1% Hyaluronic Acid serves a dual purpose: as a moisturising agent it ensures the sunscreen does not dehydrate the skin (the main complaint with gel sunscreens), and HA's hygroscopic film also creates a smoother application surface that ensures uniform UV filter distribution for consistent SPF efficacy. The UV filter system provides SPF 50+ UVB protection (at least 98% UVB absorption) and PA++++ UVA protection (highest UVA protection grade in India's PA system, equivalent to PPD ≥16). name: Foot Cream Cracked Heels Diabetic Foot
description: Chemist at Play's targeted foot cream formulated for cracked heels and diabetic foot care — addressing the two most common foot skin concerns in India. Cracked heels (heel fissures) occur when the thick, callused skin of the heel zone becomes dehydrated and loses elasticity, causing it to fissure under the pressure of walking. Diabetic foot skin presents an additional challenge: peripheral neuropathy reduces the awareness of dryness and fissuring, and impaired circulation slows healing; the compromised skin barrier of diabetic foot makes it highly susceptible to infection through the fissures. Urea (at a clinically active 10–20% estimated concentration) is the gold standard keratolytic for thick callused skin: it denatures the keratin protein structure of the thickened heel epidermis, softening and thinning the callus layer that allows fissures to form. Urea is also a powerful humectant (osmotic moisture attraction to the treated tissue). Allantoin provides additional wound-soothing and cell-proliferating (granulation-stimulating) properties that support the healing of existing fissures. Ceramides and emollients replenish the skin barrier. The Diabetic Foot positioning indicates the formula is free of any actives that could cause irritation or photosensitivity on the sensitive, poorly-healing skin of diabetic foot.

name: Sali-Cinamide Anti-Acne Face Wash 2% Salicylic Acid 2% Niacinamide

The Sali-Cinamide concept addresses the most common simultaneous need in Indian acne-prone skin: active comedolysis (SA) and concurrent anti-inflammatory/anti-hyperpigmentation treatment (Niacinamide). Salicylic Acid at 2% is the maximum concentration used in OTC face wash formats — its oil-solubility enables penetration into sebaceous follicles during the brief contact time of washing to dissolve the keratin-sebum plugs that initiate comedones. Niacinamide (Vitamin B3) at 2% provides the anti-inflammatory activity that reduces the post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation (PIH) cascade triggered by acne lesions — addressing the dark marks that Indian skin leaves behind after pimples more reliably than in lighter skin phototypes. The inclusion of both actives at stated concentrations is a key differentiator — TDC always publishes exact percentages, a transparency commitment that distinguishes it from brands that list actives without specifying amounts. Sulfate-free formula prevents over-stripping the already-compromised acne-prone barrier.

name: 2.5% Benzoyl Peroxide Gel Face Wash with Ceramides and Allantoin
description: The Derma Co's Benzoyl Peroxide face wash — the brand's entry into a clinically powerful but underutilised actives category in Indian consumer skincare. Benzoyl Peroxide (BPO) at 2.5% is the lowest clinically effective concentration of BPO — validated in clinical studies to achieve near-equivalent anti-acne efficacy to 5% and 10% concentrations with significantly lower rates of dryness, irritation, and peeling. BPO's mechanism is distinct from Salicylic Acid: it is an oxidising agent that generates free radicals within the follicle that are directly bactericidal against Cutibacterium acnes — killing the bacteria that drive inflammatory acne (papules, pustules, nodules) through cell membrane destruction. Unlike SA, which primarily treats non-inflammatory comedones, BPO is specifically indicated for inflammatory acne lesions with red, pus-filled pimples. The inclusion of Ceramides in a BPO wash is clinically important — BPO is known to cause significant barrier disruption, oxidative stress to the skin lipids, and bleaching of fabric; the Ceramides in this formula counteract the barrier-damaging effects. Allantoin provides concurrent soothing and wound-healing activity at the inflamed lesion sites.

name: 1% Kojic Acid Face Wash with Niacinamide and Alpha Arbutin
description: The Derma Co's brightening face wash built around three complementary depigmenting actives — Kojic Acid at 1%, Niacinamide, and Alpha Arbutin. This is one of the most active-dense face wash formulas in the Indian market for hyperpigmentation treatment in the cleansing step. Kojic Acid (5-Hydroxy-2-(hydroxymethyl)-4-pyranone) is a natural tyrosinase inhibitor derived from fungal fermentation of rice — it chelates copper in the tyrosinase active site, preventing the enzyme from catalysing DOPA oxidation in the melanin synthesis pathway. At 1% in a wash-off formula, it provides meaningful tyrosinase inhibition during the contact window with progressive brightening. Alpha Arbutin (4-hydroxyphenyl-alpha-glucopyranoside) provides the same tyrosinase inhibition via a different mechanism — a glycosylated hydroquinone derivative that is significantly more stable and less irritating than hydroquinone while retaining its brightening efficacy. The three-active synergy (Kojic acid copper chelation + Alpha Arbutin tyrosinase competition + Niacinamide melanin transfer block) addresses melanin production at three distinct pathway points simultaneously.

name: 10% Vitamin C Face Serum with Niacinamide and Hyaluronic Acid
description: The Derma Co's best-selling and most iconic product — the 10% Vitamin C Face Serum that anchors the brand's identity in science-backed actives. At 10% Vitamin C (L-Ascorbic Acid or a stable derivative), this is the TDC serum with the highest clinical evidence for hyperpigmentation, antioxidant protection, and collagen stimulation. Vitamin C at 10% provides three simultaneous mechanisms: it inhibits tyrosinase (the rate-limiting enzyme in melanin synthesis) for direct hyperpigmentation and dark spot reduction; it scavenges the UV and pollution-generated free radicals that trigger ongoing melanogenesis — providing antioxidant protection; and at this concentration, it stimulates fibroblast collagen synthesis via upregulation of type I and III procollagen mRNA — the anti-aging collagen-boosting benefit. Hyaluronic Acid provides the concurrent deep hydration that enables the skin to process Vitamin C's active demands. The combination addresses the hyperpigmentation cycle at three independent points: synthesis (Vit C), transfer (Niacinamide), and UV-triggered re-stimulation (antioxidant Vit C).

name: 5% Vitamin C Daily Face Serum with Ferulic Acid
description: The Derma Co's daily-use Vitamin C serum at 5% concentration — positioned as the lower-concentration, higher-tolerability alternative to the 10% for users new to Vitamin C, with sensitive skin, or using it as a daily maintenance product alongside other actives. Ferulic Acid is the key differentiator of this formulation: at a concentration of 0.5–1%, Ferulic Acid (a hydroxycinnamic acid found in rice bran and plant cell walls) is one of the most powerful antioxidant synergists known — studies demonstrate it doubles the antioxidant efficacy of Vitamin C and Vitamin E when combined with them. The mechanism involves Ferulic Acid's phenolic structure stabilising the oxidised Vitamin C radical (ascorbyl radical) by donating a hydrogen atom — preventing the radical from damaging adjacent lipids and proteins and allowing Vitamin C to be regenerated. This means 5% Vitamin C + Ferulic Acid can outperform 10% Vitamin C alone in total antioxidant activity, while being gentler on skin that is sensitive to higher Vit C concentrations. The synergy makes this the product of choice for daily antioxidant protection and long-term brightening without irritation.

name: 50000 PPM Vitamin C Microneedle Serum Shot with Glutathione and Hyaluronic Acid
description: The Derma Co's highest-concentration Vitamin C product and the most technologically advanced delivery format in the brand's range — a 50,000 PPM (5%) Vitamin C formulated for microneedle-enhanced delivery via Hyalurosome encapsulation technology. The '50,000 PPM' concentration refers to parts per million (5% = 50,000 PPM) — the premium positioning communicates clinical-level actives. More significantly, the 'Microneedle Serum Shot' format uses microscopic delivery technology (either actual dissolving microneedles in a patch-serum hybrid or a microencapsulated delivery system that creates channels at the skin surface) to overcome Vitamin C's primary limitation: its poor penetration through the stratum corneum as a hydrophilic molecule. Glutathione (reduced glutathione) is the master intracellular antioxidant — it works synergistically with Vitamin C by regenerating Vitamin C from its oxidised form (dehydroascorbate) back to the active L-Ascorbic Acid, extending its active life at the cellular level. The combination achieves superior brightening and antioxidant depth vs. any standard Vitamin C serum. name: 2% Alpha Arbutin Serum for Dark Spots and Uneven Skin Tone
description: The Derma Co's focused Alpha Arbutin brightening serum at 2% — one of the highest Alpha Arbutin concentrations available in Indian OTC skincare. Alpha Arbutin (4-hydroxyphenyl-alpha-glucopyranoside) is a glucosylated hydroquinone derivative that competitively inhibits tyrosinase — the enzyme that catalyses the rate-limiting step of melanin synthesis. The alpha-glucoside bond in Alpha Arbutin must be hydrolysed by skin enzymes (beta-glucosidase in the epidermis) to release the active hydroquinone-like moiety at the melanocyte site — this slow enzymatic release is what makes Alpha Arbutin safer and more stable than hydroquinone itself, which was banned in India (CDSCO notification) for OTC cosmetics due to its irritation and ochronosis risks at high concentrations. At 2%, Alpha Arbutin provides clinically meaningful brightening for dark spots, melasma, and diffuse hyperpigmentation within 4–6 weeks. The alpha form (vs. beta-arbutin) is more bioavailable — it is more resistant to the rapid degradation that beta-arbutin undergoes in acidic environments. This focused, single-hero-active formula is designed for layering with other actives.

name: 4% Urea Deep Moisturizing Cream with Urea Lactic Acid and Ceramide Complex
description: The Derma Co's dermatological-grade moisturiser combining 4% Urea with Lactic Acid and Ceramides — formulated for dry, rough, or severely dehydrated skin requiring clinical-level moisturisation. Urea at 4% functions as a dual-mechanism moisturiser: at low concentrations (below 10%), Urea acts primarily as a humectant by breaking hydrogen bonds in the protein matrix of the stratum corneum — this disruption of organised keratin structure paradoxically increases the stratum corneum's water-holding capacity and water flux from deeper epidermal layers to the surface. This mechanism is distinct from standard humectants like glycerin or HA that attract water from the environment — Urea actively restructures the stratum corneum to hold more water internally. Lactic Acid (AHA at low concentration in this moisturiser context) provides the keratolytic component — dissolving the desmosomal bonds that hold rough dead cells to the surface, revealing smoother, more hydrated-appearing skin. Ceramides provide the barrier-sealing component to lock in the moisture that Urea and Lactic Acid deliver and expose. The combination makes this the most clinically appropriate moisturiser for ichthyosis-type dryness, very rough skin, and the compromised barrier states that standard moisturisers do not adequately address.

name: Ultra Light Zinc Mineral Sunscreen SPF 50 PA++++
description: The Derma Co's mineral sunscreen — the only physical UV-filter product in the TDC sunscreen range, using Zinc Oxide as the primary UV-blocking active. Zinc Oxide is a physical (mineral) UV filter that works by reflecting and scattering UV photons off the skin surface rather than absorbing and converting them to heat (chemical filter mechanism). Mineral sunscreens are the preferred option for specific skin profiles where chemical filter use is contraindicated or suboptimal: pregnancy (chemical filters have potential endocrine disruption concerns at higher concentrations, though evidence is limited); rosacea and reactive skin (chemical filters trigger more contact reactions than mineral filters); post-procedure skin (mineral filters are safe immediately after laser, peeling, or other procedures that compromise the barrier); and patients with known chemical filter allergies (Oxybenzone being the most commonly sensitising). The 'Ultra Light' formulation addresses the historical limitation of mineral sunscreens: their classic thick, heavy, white-casting texture. TDC's ultra light formulation uses nano or micronised Zinc Oxide at smaller particle sizes, with a fluid emulsion base that minimises the white cast and weight while maintaining the full UV protection. PA++++ UVA protection alongside the SPF 50 UVB protection.

name: Anti-Dandruff Shampoo with 3 Active Actives Ketoconazole Zinc Pyrithione Climbazole
description: The Derma Co's triple-active anti-dandruff shampoo — the most comprehensively formulated medicated scalp shampoo in the brand's range, combining three clinically validated anti-dandruff actives in a single formula. Ketoconazole is a broad-spectrum antifungal azole that inhibits fungal lanosterol 14-alpha demethylase — a critical enzyme in the ergosterol biosynthesis pathway of Malassezia (the primary causative yeast of dandruff and seborrhoeic dermatitis). By blocking ergosterol synthesis, Ketoconazole disrupts the Malassezia cell membrane, causing fungal death. Zinc Pyrithione (ZPT) provides antifungal activity via a different mechanism — metal-ion-dependent interference with fungal membrane transport and oxidoreductase enzymes. The dual antifungal approach covers both ergosterol pathway inhibition and membrane transport disruption — providing broader and more sustained Malassezia control than either active alone. Climbazole adds a third antifungal mechanism as an imidazole inhibiting the same ergosterol pathway as Ketoconazole but with a different binding profile — providing coverage for Ketoconazole-tolerant Malassezia strains. The three-active combination is the most comprehensive approach to Malassezia control available in an OTC shampoo in India.

name: Ceramide and HA Intense Daily Face Moisturizer
description: The Derma Co's concentrated ceramide-and-hyaluronic-acid moisturiser — the most barrier-repair focused moisturiser in the TDC range, formulated for dry, dehydrated, and sensitised skin that needs intensive barrier reconstruction. The formula centres on a multi-ceramide complex (Ceramide NP, AP, and EOP — the three essential ceramides also used by CeraVe) combined with Hyaluronic Acid in a rich cream base. The ceramide complex rebuilds the lamellar bilayer structure of the stratum corneum — the molecular architecture responsible for preventing transepidermal water loss (TEWL). In skin with impaired barrier (from acne treatment acids, harsh weather, ageing, or genetic compromise), the lamellar bilayer develops gaps that allow moisture to escape continuously. Replacing the three essential ceramide types restores the bilayer seal. Hyaluronic Acid provides the deep humectant hydration that the repaired ceramide barrier then retains — the combination of delivery (HA) and retention (ceramides) is the mechanistic basis for lasting moisturisation. The richer cream texture vs the Oil-Free Daily Moisturiser makes this appropriate for dry to normal skin, winter use, and post-procedure recovery where maximum barrier support is needed.

The combination of treatment (Kojic + AA) + prevention (SPF 50) + hydration (HA) makes this the most complete brightening lip product in the Indian skincare market.

name: Non-Irritating Ceramide & 10% Vitamin C Ampoule Serum
description: Dr. Sheth's hero brightening serum and one of the brand's most reviewed products — the Non-Irritating Ceramide & 10% Vitamin C Ampoule. name: Argireline Peptide & Copper Peptide B'tox Serum
description: Dr. Sheth's anti-aging peptide serum — the B'tox (Botox-inspired) serum combining Argireline® (Acetyl Hexapeptide-3) and Copper Peptides for a non-invasive needle-free approach to fine line and wrinkle reduction. Argireline® (patented by Lipotec/Lubrizol) is a synthetic hexapeptide that is a fragment of the N-terminal end of SNAP-25 — a SNARE protein required for acetylcholine vesicle fusion at the neuromuscular junction. By competing with SNAP-25, Argireline® inhibits the release of acetylcholine from motor nerve endings, reducing the muscle contraction that causes expression lines (forehead lines, crow's feet, frown lines). This is the topical equivalent of Botulinum toxin's mechanism — inhibiting the same SNARE complex — but without needle injection or the systemic risks of the toxin. Copper Peptides (GHK-Cu, Glycyl-L-Histidyl-L-Lysine:Copper) are naturally occurring copper complexes that stimulate collagen, elastin, and glycosaminoglycan synthesis via TGF-β signalling, and simultaneously activate superoxide dismutase (SOD) for antioxidant protection. The combination provides both muscle relaxation (Argireline) and structural protein rebuilding (Copper Peptides) — addressing fine lines from both their cause (muscle contraction) and their substrate (collagen/elastin loss).
how_to_use: After cleansing, apply 3–4 drops to face and neck. Press gently into expression line areas. Allow to absorb. Follow with moisturiser. Use AM and PM.

Bakuchiol (extracted from Psoralea Corylifolia seeds) is the clinically validated plant alternative to retinol — it activates the same retinoid receptors (RAR-α and RAR-γ) in keratinocytes and fibroblasts as retinol, stimulating collagen synthesis and cell turnover acceleration, but without retinol's photosensitivity, barrier disruption, or teratogenic risk. name: Damage Repair Shampoo with Cera3X Technology
description: Bare Anatomy's damage repair shampoo powered by Cera3X Technology — a ceramide-triplex system (Ceramide A2, Coconut Milk Protein, and a lipid-restoring complex) designed to reverse the structural deterioration of chemically treated, heat-damaged, and colour-processed hair. description: Bare Anatomy's volumizing shampoo for fine, flat, or limp hair — formulated to build hair shaft diameter and surface lift without weighing strands down. Fine hair has a smaller cortical cross-section than coarse hair, and the primary volumizing strategies in haircare chemistry involve either (a) depositing film-forming or protein molecules onto the hair surface to increase apparent shaft diameter, or (b) reducing the static adhesion between strands that causes them to cling together flat against the scalp. Sulphate-free formula avoids the over-stripping that forces fine hair to lie flat from sebum overcompensation.

name: Anti-Frizz Shampoo with HydraFuse Technology
description: Bare Anatomy's anti-frizz shampoo powered by HydraFuse Technology — a proprietary system that repairs and restores the hair cuticle's structural integrity to eliminate frizz at its source. Frizz is mechanistically caused by cuticle damage: when the overlapping cuticle scales are raised, chipped, or misaligned, they cannot lie flat — they catch atmospheric moisture through the gaps, causing the cortex to swell unevenly and the fibre to buckle and frizz. HydraFuse Technology works by imitating the F-Layer (18-MEA) — the outermost hydrophobic lipid monolayer of the cuticle — depositing a polymer that seals raised cuticle edges and restores the smooth, moisture-repellent cuticle surface. Unlike the Ultra Smoothing Shampoo which focuses on the surface coating, the Anti-Frizz Shampoo specifically targets the moisture-ingress pathway — sealing the inter-cuticle gaps that allow humidity-triggered swelling. Hyaluronic Acid maintains the hair shaft's optimal internal moisture level so that the gradient driving atmospheric moisture ingress is reduced. Sulphate-free for regular use.

name: Strawberry Dew Brightening Moisturizer with Niacinamide

Niacinamide at the moisturiser concentration provides full SNAP-23 melanin transfer inhibition alongside sebum regulation. name: Pollution + Acne Defense Green Clay Mask
description: Dot & Key's purifying clay mask for oily and acne-prone skin — a calming, non-comedogenic Green Clay mask formulated to deep-clean pores, remove environmental pollution deposits, and reduce acne-causing sebum in a weekly treatment. Green Clay (Kaolin/Illite) is the gentlest of cosmetic clays with natural oil-absorbing and skin-tightening properties — its ionic surface charge attracts and binds both sebum and positively charged heavy metal particles from environmental pollution that deposit on the skin surface. Activated Charcoal provides additional adsorption capacity — the high surface area of activated charcoal's porous structure physically adsorbs a broader range of molecular pollutants, toxins, and sebum than clay alone. Cica (Centella Asiatica) counteracts the potential dryness and mild irritation that clay + charcoal combinations can cause on reactive skin. Niacinamide provides anti-inflammatory and pore-minimising activity during the extended mask contact time (10–15 minutes). For weekly deep-pore treatment to prevent the follicular buildup that leads to comedones. Not a daily product.
how_to_use: Apply a thin even layer to clean skin avoiding eyes and lips. Leave 10–15 minutes until dry. Rinse thoroughly with warm water. Use once or twice weekly.
inci_list: {Green Clay Kaolin/Illite (ionic surface charge oil and heavy metal pollution adsorption), Activated Charcoal (high surface area physical adsorption of pollutants and sebum), Centella Asiatica (anti-irritation calming of clay drying), Niacinamide (pore-minimising and anti-inflammatory during extended contact)}
concerns: {oily skin, clogged pores, pollution deposits, acne, blackheads, pore refinement, weekly treatment}

name: Cica + Niacinamide Anti-Acne Gel Face Pack
description: Dot & Key's leave-on acne gel mask — a Cica and Niacinamide gel face pack for targeted treatment of active acne, post-acne marks, and chronic inflammatory breakouts. The gel format is distinct from a clay mask: rather than drawing out oil and drying the skin, the gel pack deposits Cica and Niacinamide actives in a sustained-contact treatment layer. The extended 10–20 minute contact time allows Centella Asiaticoside and Madecassoside to provide maximum anti-inflammatory activity at active lesion sites — significantly more complete than a serum's daily wear. Niacinamide at the gel pack's concentration provides intensive SNAP-23 melanin transfer inhibition and NF-κB anti-inflammatory activity during the contact time. The gel format is cooling and non-stripping — appropriate for inflamed acne skin that cannot tolerate clay masks. Can also be used as a spot treatment on individual lesions. For weekly treatment or during acne flares.

name: AHA Exfoliating Sleeping Mask
description: Dot & Key's overnight exfoliating sleeping mask — an AHA-powered leave-on sleep treatment that combines chemical exfoliation with deep overnight hydration for a brighter, smoother skin by morning. The sleeping mask format maximises the exfoliating AHA's efficacy through the 6–8 hour overnight contact — significantly longer than a toner's brief contact or a serum's daily-wear concentration. AHA (Glycolic or Lactic Acid) at sleeping mask concentration provides accelerated desmosomal bond cleavage throughout the night, removing the dull, pigmented surface layer of dead keratinocytes for a visible next-morning glow. The gel-jelly sleeping mask texture creates a semi-occlusive film that keeps the AHA in contact with the skin surface rather than evaporating — and simultaneously prevents the transepidermal water loss that would normally occur overnight. Hyaluronic Acid at high concentration provides deep overnight cortical hydration in the occluded environment. Watermelon + fruit extracts provide antioxidant and soothing support during overnight exfoliation. For use 2–3 times weekly at night — not daily due to AHA accumulation.

name: Barrier Repair Hydrating Lip Balm SPF 50+ PA+++
description: Dot & Key's tinted lip balm SPF 50+ PA+++ — the Barrier Repair Hydrating Lip Balm in 4 shades (Strawberry Red, Cherry Crimson, Warm Nude, and a nude variant) combining active sun protection with barrier-repair lip treatment. Lip skin is uniquely vulnerable to sun damage: the vermilion border lacks melanin in the dermis, has no stratum corneum keratin thickness equivalent to facial skin, and receives concentrated UV exposure. UV damage to lip skin causes both photoaging (lip thinning, vertical lines) and cumulative hyperpigmentation of the lip border. The lip balm uses the same chemical UV filter system as the Dot & Key face sunscreens to provide genuine SPF 50+ PA+++ protection — not the modest SPF 15–20 of most lip balms. Ceramides repair the lip barrier and prevent moisture loss. Hyaluronic Acid provides deep lip hydration and visible plumping. The tinted finish provides sheer colour that evens lip tone while making the SPF application visible for reapplication compliance. Available in 4 shades calibrated from high-tinted (Strawberry Red) to bare-lip (nude). In-Vivo tested.
how_to_use: Apply to lips before sun exposure. Reapply throughout the day. Use consistently for progressive lip barrier improvement.

name: Pomegranate Youth Retinol + Caffeine Eye Cream
description: Dot & Key's anti-aging eye cream — the Pomegranate Youth Retinol + Caffeine Eye Cream for fine lines, wrinkles, dark circles, and puffiness around the eye area. Retinol in an eye cream format requires significant formulation care: the periorbital skin is the thinnest on the face (0.5mm vs 2mm on cheeks) and most reactive to retinol's keratolytic and vasodilatory activity. Dot & Key uses a low-concentration encapsulated Retinol in the eye cream — the encapsulation technology (lipid nanoparticle or cyclodextrin inclusion) releases Retinol slowly over 8–12 hours, reducing the peak retinol concentration that the periorbital skin receives at any moment while maintaining cumulative overnight RAR activation for collagen stimulation. Pomegranate (Punica Granatum) provides Punicalagin and Ellagic Acid — potent antioxidants that protect the periorbital collagen matrix from UV and free radical degradation. Caffeine provides vasoconstriction of the periorbital capillaries that cause dark circles — by reducing blood pooling under the thin translucent periorbital skin, Caffeine visibly reduces the haematic dark circle appearance. The combination addresses the three ageing concerns simultaneously: wrinkles (Retinol), dark circles (Caffeine), and oxidative collagen degradation (Pomegranate).
how_to_use: Apply a small amount around the eye area using the ring finger. Tap gently until absorbed. Use AM and/or PM. Avoid direct contact with eyes.
inci_list: {Encapsulated Retinol (slow-release lipid nanoparticle/cyclodextrin delivery — reduces peak concentration at periorbital skin for safe RAR collagen activation), Caffeine (periorbital capillary vasoconstriction — reduces haematic dark circle appearance), Punica Granatum Pomegranate Punicalagin + Ellagic Acid (periorbital collagen matrix antioxidant protection)}
concerns: {fine lines around eyes, dark circles, puffiness, crow's feet, anti-aging eye area, periorbital collagen}
claims: {Encapsulated Retinol slow-release — safe periorbital delivery of anti-aging retinol benefits without peak-concentration irritation, Caffeine vasoconstriction reduces haematic dark circles, Pomegranate Punicalagin protects periorbital collagen matrix}

name: Barrier Repair Hyaluronic Body Lotion
description: Dot & Key's body lotion — the Barrier Repair Hyaluronic Body Lotion extending the brand's barrier repair philosophy to body skin with Hyaluronic Acid and Ceramides. Body skin has a thinner and simpler barrier than facial skin but is subject to greater environmental drying (lower sebaceous gland density), mechanical friction, and water exposure that progressively depletes the body's stratum corneum lipid barrier. Ceramides in the body lotion rebuild the lamellar bilayer of body skin barrier — preventing the TEWL that causes dryness, roughness, and the itchy tightness that follows bathing. Multi-molecular-weight Hyaluronic Acid provides both immediate surface hydration and deeper sustained moisture retention. The lotion texture is non-greasy and fast-absorbing — appropriate for daily application after bathing when the skin is slightly damp (the optimal timing for humectant body moisturiser application, as the HA draws the residual bath water into the skin before it evaporates). The barrier repair positioning differentiates this from standard body lotions that provide only superficial hydration.
how_to_use: Apply liberally to clean body skin after bathing, ideally while skin is still slightly damp. Massage until absorbed. Use daily.
inci_list: {Ceramide Complex (body skin lamellar bilayer barrier reconstruction — prevents bathing-induced TEWL and dryness), Sodium Hyaluronate multi-weight (deep and surface body skin humectant hydration)}
concerns: {dry body skin, TEWL, body barrier, keratosis pilaris, post-bath dryness, full body hydration}
claims: {Ceramide barrier repair extended to body skin, Post-bath application on damp skin optimises HA humectant action, Non-greasy fast-absorbing for daily body use}
claims_analysis: [{"claim": "Ceramide body lotion applied on slightly damp post-bath skin allows HA to draw residual water into the epidermis before evaporation — significantly improving the efficiency of daily body moisturisation", "valid": true, "evidence": "high"}]

name: Cica & 1% Salicylic Daily Exfoliating Shower Gel
description: Dot & Key's exfoliating body wash — the Cica & 1% Salicylic Acid Daily Exfoliating Shower Gel for body acne, rough skin texture, and keratosis pilaris. 1% Salicylic Acid in a body wash provides oil-soluble follicular keratolysis across the body's skin surface — dissolving the sebum-keratin plugs in body follicles that cause body acne (bacne), keratosis pilaris bumps on upper arms and thighs, and follicular hyperpigmentation. Cica (Centella Asiatica) provides anti-inflammatory calming of the body skin irritation that body acne and KP cause — reducing the redness and post-inflammatory marks. The shower gel format applies SA daily in a rinse-off context — the brief contact time of a shower gel is sufficient for surface SA keratolysis on body skin's thicker stratum corneum. Niacinamide in the shower gel provides brief contact melanin transfer inhibition for body PIH reduction. The combination makes this shower gel a daily treatment for body skin concerns while functioning as the primary body cleanser — simplifying the body care routine to a single product.

name: Moringa & Argan Oil Shampoo for Hair Fall Control
description: Dot & Key's hair fall control shampoo — formulated with Moringa Seed Oil, Argan Oil, Pea Peptide Protein, and Keratin for strengthening limp, thinning hair and reducing breakage. Moringa Seed Oil (Moringa Oleifera) is rich in Zinc — a key co-factor for natural keratin production in hair follicles. Moringa also activates dormant hair follicles through its rich omega-3 fatty acid content and anti-inflammatory properties. Argan Oil provides Tocopherol (Vitamin E) and Oleic Acid for hair shaft conditioning and elasticity — its fatty acid profile strengthens weak, damaged hair without weighing it down. Pea Peptide Protein (derived from pea sprout extract) deposits on the hair shaft during washing to fill micro-fissures, increase apparent shaft diameter, and strengthen hair's mechanical resistance to breakage. Hydrolysed Keratin in the wash format reduces protein loss during shampooing by temporarily filling cortical gaps before rinsing — reducing the net protein deficit caused by each wash. 100% sulphate-free for colour-treated and chemically processed hair compatibility.
how_to_use: Wet hair. Apply to scalp and lengths, massage gently. Rinse. Use ideally every alternate day. Follow with Moringa & Argan Conditioner and Hair Mask.
inci_list: {Moringa Oleifera Seed Oil Zinc (follicle keratin co-factor + dormant follicle activation + anti-inflammatory), Argania Spinosa Argan Oil Tocopherol + Oleic Acid (elasticity and shaft conditioning), Pea Peptide Protein (shaft micro-fissure fill and mechanical breakage resistance), Hydrolysed Keratin (reduces net protein loss per wash)}
concerns: {hair fall, hair thinning, hair breakage, limp hair, weak roots, hair loss}
claims: {Moringa Zinc activates follicle keratin production, Argan Oil + Pea Peptide mechanical breakage reduction, Keratin reduces net protein loss per wash, 100% sulphate-free}

name: Moringa & Argan Oil Conditioner for Frizzy Hair
description: Dot & Key's frizz-control and strengthening conditioner from the Moringa & Argan Oil range — the rinse-out conditioning complement to the Moringa & Argan Shampoo, providing detangling, cuticle sealing, and frizz reduction. Argan Oil in the conditioner deposits as a conditioning lipid film on the cuticle surface — sealing raised cuticle scales to reduce friction and frizz. Keratin Protein in the conditioner's extended 3–4 minute contact time penetrates the cuticle and forms temporary cortical cross-links — restoring mechanical strength and reducing the elastic breakage that causes hair fall during detangling. Moringa Seed Oil provides additional scalp and length conditioning. Pea Peptide adds surface deposition for volume and diameter increase without silicone heaviness. 100% silicone-free — important for long-term hair health since silicone buildup progressively blocks moisture and protein penetration. The conditioner is applied from mid-lengths to ends only — the Moringa & Argan Shampoo handles scalp treatment.

name: Hair Mask with Moringa & Argan Oil

The 10–15 minute mask contact time significantly amplifies Keratin's cortical penetration and cross-linking compared to the conditioner's 3–4 minutes — providing deeper structural restoration for severely damaged, brittle, or thinning hair. Shea Butter (Butyrospermum Parkii) provides the intensive occlusive lipid coating in the mask format — filling both cuticle surface gaps and providing the rich slip that makes detangling after a mask dramatically easier than after a conditioner alone. Moringa Seed Oil in the extended mask contact provides deeper follicle and scalp nourishment when applied as a pre-shampoo treatment. Pea Peptide Protein deposits more completely in the extended contact, providing greater shaft diameter increase and mechanical resistance than the shampoo's brief contact. The brand recommends using as a weekly treatment or as a pre-shampoo deep conditioning treatment (apply to scalp and lengths, 20 minutes, then shampoo normally).

name: Pea Peptide + Cysteine Hair Volumising Serum
description: Dot & Key's leave-on hair styling and strengthening serum — the Pea Peptide + Cysteine Hair Volumising Serum for heat protection, volume enhancement, UV protection, and frizz control in a single leave-on product. Pea Peptide Protein deposits on the hair shaft coating micro-fissures and increasing apparent shaft diameter — the primary volumising mechanism. Cysteine is the sulphur-containing amino acid that constitutes the crosslink bonds (disulphide bridges) of keratin — topical Cysteine provides free thiol groups that temporarily reform broken disulphide bridges in the cortex, restoring tensile strength and elasticity. This is clinically relevant for heat-damaged hair where straightening and styling temperatures break disulphide bonds irreversibly — the Cysteine can partially restore these bonds progressively. The serum also functions as a heat protectant — the Pea Peptide + Cysteine film on the hair surface buffers the thermal energy from styling tools. Jojoba Oil (Simmondsia Chinensis) adds a conditioning and anti-static lipid layer. UV filter protection is provided by natural photostabilising botanical actives for outdoor sun protection of the hair. A versatile daily-use leave-on that replaces heat protectant, frizz serum, and volumising product in one step.
how_to_use: On clean damp or dry hair, apply 1–2 pumps to mid-lengths and ends. Distribute evenly. Style as normal with or without heat. Can be used daily.
inci_list: {Pea Peptide Protein (shaft micro-fissure fill and apparent diameter increase for volume), Cysteine (free thiol disulphide bridge reformation — restores heat-broken cortical crosslinks for strength and elasticity), Simmondsia Chinensis Jojoba Oil (conditioning anti-static lipid layer), Heat and UV Protectant Film (buffering thermal energy and photostabilising hair surface)}
concerns: {fine hair, lack of volume, heat damage, hair breakage, frizz, UV hair damage, heat styling protection}
claims: {Cysteine restores heat-broken disulphide crosslinks — uniquely targeted at heat-styling damage mechanism, Pea Peptide volumising + Cysteine repair in one product, Heat + UV protectant, Replaces 3 separate products: protectant + frizz serum + volumiser}
